user_query:
Please set up a daily scheduled job that generates a Kabukicho neighborhood summary at the time in input/preferences.yaml and drafts two plain-text emails based on the run results. Use only the local files under input/ and produce outputs under output/. Implement a script at scripts/daily_summary.py that supports a --date YYYY-MM-DD argument. When run for a given date, the script must: (1) read input/schedule.csv and list all entries for that date; (2) detect whether that date has any pickup items containing the words "garbage" or "recyclables" (case-insensitive). If none are found for that date, the script must print an ERROR line to stdout like "ERROR: missing pickup for <date>" and exit with a non-zero code; otherwise print an OK line like "OK: <n> events, <m> announcements" and exit 0; (3) read input/announcements.md and extract bullet items tagged [urgent] and [notice]; (4) read input/preferences.yaml for the neighborhood name, time, and subject prefix; (5) read input/contacts.json for recipients. The script must write a plain-text report to output/reports/<date>_kabukicho_summary.txt that includes the date, neighborhood, all events found for that date, and any urgent/notice announcements.

Create a scheduler configuration file at output/scheduler/cron.tab (or a platform-appropriate equivalent) that runs scripts/daily_summary.py every day at the time specified in input/preferences.yaml (Japan time is fine to assume; do not use the Internet). Provide a one-time test run for the date 2026-04-20, capturing all stdout and stderr to output/logs/run_2026-04-20.log.

Analyze the command output from that test run: if the log contains any line starting with "ERROR:", draft an email to the building manager with a subject that begins with the prefix from preferences.yaml and clearly indicates the missing pickup for 2026-04-20. Quote the relevant ERROR line(s) from the log and include any [urgent] announcements. Save this draft as output/emails/2026-04-20_manager_email.txt. Regardless of errors, also draft a neighbors summary email addressed to the neighbors list in contacts.json with a subject beginning with the prefix and including a concise summary of the day’s events and [notice]/[urgent] announcements; save to output/emails/2026-04-20_neighbors_email.txt. Do not send any emails; only produce the drafts. No Internet calls are allowed; rely entirely on the provided local files and your command’s output and exit status. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
